---
title: Repository layout
---

A Quarto course repository usually separates authored content, build
configuration, support resources, and rendered output. Learn the role of each
area before deciding where to edit.

## Common roles

| Role | Typical files or folders | Edit during ordinary content work? |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Source documents | `index.qmd`, `lessons/*.qmd`, `exercises/*.qmd` | Yes |
| Assets and data | `figures/`, `media/`, `datasets/`, `files/` | Yes, when referenced content changes |
| Quarto configuration | `_quarto.yml`, `_quarto-<profile>.yml` | Only for rendering/navigation changes |
| Extensions and filters | `_extensions/`, `filters/` | Only for feature/infrastructure changes |
| Reproducible tools | `pixi.toml`, `pyproject.toml`, `pixi.lock` | Only for tool/task changes |
| Published build | `.github/workflows/` | Only for deployment changes |
| Rendered output | `_site/`, `_output/`, `_site-combined/` | Inspect, generally do not edit |

## Website-style project

```text
repository/
├── pixi.toml
├── .github/workflows/
└── course_notes/
    ├── _quarto.yml
    ├── _quarto-exercise.yml       # optional profile
    ├── _quarto-solution.yml       # optional profile
    ├── exercises/                 # source documents
    ├── files/                     # downloads or assets
    ├── _extensions/               # configured authoring features
    └── _site/                     # rendered website
```

## Notebook/report-style project

```text
repository/
├── pixi.toml
└── lab_report/
    ├── _quarto.yml
    ├── _quarto-student.yml
    ├── _quarto-instructor.yml
    ├── report_1.qmd               # canonical source document
    ├── filters/
    └── _output/                   # HTML, PDF, and notebook output
```

The repository may use different names or combine website and notebook
outputs. The useful distinction is between maintained source and derived
output, not the exact directory layout.
